Name: Pattern Recognition - PE

Text: Case 2002 from 2024 04 01 09 05 to 2024 04 02 11 00 total 25h55 about 25 92 hours longer than case 2001 by about 25 hours

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Cases with performance issues 2002 2003 2005 especially 2005 Cases 2001 and 2004 have short durations under 2 hours

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: High complexity leads to multiple requests for additional documents Cases 2003 and 2005 are High and took longer Medium complexity case 2002 also required a request and was longer than low complexity cases

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 2002 major delay is between Request Additional Documents at 14 00 on 04 01 and Approve at 10 00 on 04 02 about 20 hours suggesting waiting after the document request

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The request probably waiting for response Also maybe due to resource availability The claim needed documents and the delay may be due to Region B or medium complexity requiring additional docs

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 2003 has two Request Additional Documents events then Approve about 23 hours after the second request and Pay 17 hours after approval showing a lot of waiting time

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Perhaps Adjuster Mike performance is slower or the claim complexity is high requiring multiple document requests

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 2005 has three Request Additional Documents events with huge delays between them then approval only on 04 04 10 00 making it the longest case

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Region B may cause delays and Adjuster Lisa handling many requests may be overloaded

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Complexity High is associated with long durations because cases 2003 and 2005 are high complexity and both took far longer than the low complexity cases

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Low complexity cases 2001 and 2004 both finish in less than 2 hours suggesting low complexity claims move quickly without extra document loops

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Adjuster Lisa appears in 2002 and 2005 which are longer cases and she performs the repeated document request activities in those cases

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Adjuster Lisa also appears in case 2004 but that case is low complexity and short so the resource alone is not enough to explain delay

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Manager Bill appears for the high complexity cases 2003 and 2005 so high complexity approvals may be routed to him and may take longer

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Region B is not by itself the direct cause because case 2004 is also Region B but is short however Region B may combine with higher complexity and repeated document requests to worsen delays

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Root causes likely are high complexity causing repeated document requests limited adjuster capacity especially around repeated follow ups and possible regional capacity issues in Region B

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The most plausible driver is the Request Additional Documents loop because the longest idle periods occur between those events and the later approval

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: To mitigate increase staffing for high complexity claims especially adjusters handling document collection implement priority handling for high complexity claims and automate reminders and document collection

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Provide a complete document checklist at submission use escalation after multiple document requests and consider cross training or load balancing between regions

Name: Validation - PE
Text: We only have event timestamps so we cannot fully separate customer waiting from internal staff delay but the observed correlation strongly points to complexity and repeated document requests as the main issue with possible resource and region amplification
